content.full_text:
April 4, 1979

Ms. Moira Neuterman
Legislative Secretary
California School Board Association
916 23rd Street
Sacramento, California 95816

Dear Ms. Neuterman:

Thank you for your letter dated March 14, 1979. In the latter you
stated that a registered lobbyist for the California School Board
Association (CSBA) is involved in organizing a legislative network
seminar for CSBA members, arranging for speakers and
assisting with menu selection for the dinner and with facility
arrangements. Legislators have been invited to the reception and
dinner. The registered lobbyist is the person to be contacted in
response to the invitation to the reception and dinner. The
reception is "no host" and the dinner ($9.98 per person) will be
paid for by CSBA. You asked if the registered lobbyist was required
to disclose the event on the lobbyist report. You also asked if the
event must be reported on the lobbyist employer report.

If a lobbyist participates with an employer in arranging for a gift
of less than $10.00 to a state official, both the lobbyist and the
lobbyist's employer have a reporting obligation. The lobbyist's
employer must report the entire transaction as an "activity expense"
on Form 650.

The arrangement is not considered an "activity expense" incurred by
the lobbyist. However, the lobbyist must submit an attachment with
Form 620 disclosing the same information reported by the employer.

If I can be of further assistance, please contact me.

Sincerely,


J. RICHARD EICHMAN
Compliance Representative

California School Boards Association

March 14, 1979

Mr. Richard Eichman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Eichman:

With reference to our telephone conversation today I am enclosing a copy of the contract with the Sacramento Inn signed by Polly Lopez, member of the California School Boards Association staff. CSBA lobbyist Jeanne Werschke has been involved in organizing the legislative network seminar in as far as notifying CSBA members, arranging for speakers, and assisting Ms. Lopez in the menu selection for the dinner and with facility arrangements.

All letters to legislators were signed by CSBA Executive Director and officers. However, Jeanne Werschke's name appeared on the letters as the person to be contacted in response to the invitation to the reception and dinner. This is a "no-host" reception, and the dinner ($9.98 per person) will be paid for by the employer.

I would appreciate your opinion as to whether Jeanne Werschke needs to report any of the above, and if so, does it also have to be reported on the lobbyist employer report?

Sincerely,

Moira Neuterman
Legislative Secretary

Enc.
mn

sections.question: You asked if the registered lobbyist was required to disclose the event on the lobbyist report. You also asked if the event must be reported on the lobbyist employer report.
sections.conclusion: If a lobbyist participates with an employer in arranging for a gift of less than $10.00 to a state official, both the lobbyist and the lobbyist's employer have a reporting obligation. The lobbyist's employer must report the entire transaction as an "activity expense" on Form 650. The arrangement is not considered an "activity expense" incurred by the lobbyist. However, the lobbyist must submit an attachment with Form 620 disclosing the same information reported by the employer.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a registered lobbyist disclose their participation in organizing a legislative network seminar and dinner (costing $9.98 per person) on lobbyist and employer reports?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both the lobbyist and employer must report the dinner arrangement, but in different ways: the employer reports it as an activity expense on Form 650, while the lobbyist must submit an attachment to Form 620 with the same information.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (dated March 14, 1979) and the FPPC response (dated April 4, 1979). Formal Q/A structure is implicit rather than explicitly labeled. All key information extracted successfully.

CONCLUSION: If a lobbyist participates with an employer in arranging for a gift of less than $10.00 to a state official, both the lobbyist and the lobbyist's employer have a reporting obligation. The lobbyist's employer must report the entire transaction as an "activity expense" on Form 650. The arrangement is not considered an "activity expense" incurred by the lobbyist. However, the lobbyist must submit an attachment with Form 620 disclosing the same information reported by the employer.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a registered lobbyist who participates in arranging a legislative dinner for state officials costing less than $10 per person must disclose this on both the lobbyist report (Form 620 with attachment) and the employer report (Form 650 as an activity expense).
